---
title: "Route Punctuality Report"
author: "Dr. Sarah Okonkwo"
---

## Summary

Quarterly on-time performance held above 80% across the network, though peak-hour services on long-distance routes remained a concern.

## Method

Services were classified as on time if they arrived within five minutes of the scheduled time. Data covers all scheduled services between 1 October and 31 December 2026.

## Results

@tbl-punctuality shows the on-time rate for six routes during Q4.

::: {.column-margin}
Off-peak trains outperform peak trains across every route.
:::

| Route             | Peak (%) | Off-peak (%) |
|:------------------|:--------:|:------------:|
| Central–North     | 82       | 91           |
| Central–East      | 78       | 88           |
| Harbour–Airport   | 71       | 85           |
| West Loop         | 88       | 94           |
| Coastal Express   | 65       | 79           |
| University Line   | 90       | 96           |

: On-time rates by route and service period {#tbl-punctuality}

Off-peak services consistently outperformed peak services across all routes, with the Coastal Express showing the largest gap.

## Recommendations

- Investigate delays on the Coastal Express and Harbour–Airport routes.
- Review peak-hour scheduling on Central–East to close the gap with off-peak performance.
- Use the University Line and West Loop as benchmarks for network-wide improvement.
